meta.description: Use when building a sample-level hierarchical clustering dendrogram from a bulk expression matrix and sample annotation table, especially for QC, batch inspection, or sample similarity assessment. Trigger keywords: hierarchical clustering, dendrogram, sample QC, batch inspection, sample similarity. NOT for: differential expression testing, gene clustering heatmaps, single-cell clustering workflows.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics; hierarchical clustering uses base R dist() and hclust(); output is a distance matrix and dendrogram only; no statistical claims generated

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions; output is a sample similarity visualization; no medical claims; clearly scoped to QC and batch inspection

methodological_ground.detail: Complete linkage with Euclidean distance is a methodologically sound default for expression QC; multiple distance and linkage options correctly implemented; no causal claims

code_usability.detail: Script runs successfully in test environment; two full runs completed without error; error paths confirmed; set.seed() present; temp workspace staging is robust

functional_suitability.note: All use cases covered; 6 distance methods and 8 linkage methods; 5 output files; NOT for exclusions precise

reliability.note: 13 SKILL_* error codes; temp workspace staging prevents partial output; set.seed() present; SKILL_MEMORY_WARNING with gc() reporting; Category 3 Override: hard stops are correct design

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 278 lines; 15 referenced files well-organized across scripts/ and references/; memory usage reported at each step

agent_usability.note: When to Read External Files table maps every concern to the correct script file; step-by-step workflow in SKILL.md references the correct scripts; excellent agent learnability

human_usability.note: Trigger keywords listed in description and When to Use; NOT for exclusions prevent misfiring; maximum discoverability

security.note: No credentials; no dangerous operations; temp workspace pattern prevents output pollution; file paths from CLI only

maintainability.note: Eight-file modular structure; testthat test suite (tests/testthat/test-clustering.R); test runner (tests/run_tests.R); test data bundled; references documented

agent_specific.note: Trigger description precise with keywords and NOT-for; excellent progressive disclosure; escape hatches present; minor composability friction from dual required inputs

key_strengths: When to Read External Files table maps every operational concern to the specific script file, making agent-side debugging deterministic
Temp workspace staging pattern ensures atomic output: either all files are written or none are
13 distinct SKILL_* error codes including SKILL_MEMORY_WARNING and SKILL_PLOT_ERROR cover edge cases not seen in sibling skills
testthat test suite (tests/testthat/test-clustering.R) and test runner enable formal regression testing
Memory usage reporting with gc() at each step is unique in the collection and valuable for large datasets

recommendations.title: SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER fires at Step 3 rather than at argument validation
recommendations.problem: Invalid distance_method is not caught during argument parsing; the script loads and aligns data (Step 1 and Step 2) before detecting the invalid parameter at Step 3. This wastes time on large datasets.
recommendations.root_cause: The distance_method value is validated inside the clustering function rather than in the CLI argument validation step.
recommendations.fix: Add a validate_choice() call for --distance_method in the argument parsing section of main.R or validation_utils.R, consistent with how --linkage_method is handled, so invalid values are rejected before data loading begins.
